title: Autumn Day
lines: Lord: it is time. The summer was immense.
Lay your shadow on the sundials,
and on the meadows let the winds go loose.
Command the last fruits to be full;
give them just two more southerly days,
urge them on to fulfillment and drive
the last sweetness into the heavy wine.
Who has no house now, will never build one.
Who is alone now, will stay long alone,
will lie awake, read, write long letters,
and wander the boulevards, up and down,
restlessly, while the dry leaves blow.
linesCn: 主啊，是时候了。夏日曾经很盛大。
把你的阴影落在日晷上，
让秋风刮过田野。
让最后的果实长得丰满，
再给它们两天南方的气候，
迫使它们成熟，
把最后的甘甜酿入浓酒。
谁此时没有房屋，就不必建筑，
谁此时孤独，就永远孤独，
就醒着，读着，写着长信，
在林荫道上来回
不安地游荡，当着落叶纷飞。

title: Tonight I Can Write
lines: Tonight I can write the saddest lines.
Write, for example, 'The night is starry
and the stars are blue and shiver in the distance.'
The night wind revolves in the sky and sings.
Tonight I can write the saddest lines.
I loved her, and sometimes she loved me too.
Through nights like this one I held her in my arms.
I kissed her again and again under the endless sky.
She loved me, sometimes I loved her too.
How could one not have loved her great still eyes.
linesCn: 今夜我可以写下最哀伤的诗句。
写，譬如，'夜色布满繁星，
而星星在远方蓝莹莹地颤抖。'
夜风在天空中回旋并歌唱。
今夜我可以写下最哀伤的诗句。
我爱过她，有时她也爱我。
在如此这般的夜里我曾拥她入怀。
在无尽的天空下一次又一次地吻她。
她爱过我，有时我也爱她。
怎能不爱上她那沉静的大眼睛。
source: Twenty Love Poems and a Song of Despair

title: Possibilities
lines: I prefer movies.
I prefer cats.
I prefer the oaks along the Warta.
I prefer Dickens to Dostoyevsky.
I prefer myself liking people
to myself loving mankind.
I prefer keeping a needle and thread on hand, just in case.
I prefer the color green.
I prefer not to maintain
that reason is to blame for everything.
I prefer exceptions.
I prefer to leave early.
I prefer talking to doctors about something else.
I prefer the old fine-lined illustrations.
I prefer the absurdity of writing poems
to the absurdity of not writing poems.
linesCn: 我偏爱电影。
我偏爱猫。
我偏爱瓦尔塔河沿岸的橡树。
我偏爱狄更斯胜过陀思妥耶夫斯基。
我偏爱我对人群的喜欢
胜过我对人类的爱。
我偏爱在手边放针线，以备不时之需。
我偏爱绿色。
我偏爱不把一切
都归咎于理性。
我偏爱例外。
我偏爱及早离去。
我偏爱和医生聊些别的话题。
我偏爱那些老式的细线条插画。
我偏爱写诗的荒谬
胜过不写诗的荒谬。

title: The Just
lines: A man who cultivates his garden, as Voltaire wished.
He who is grateful for the existence of music.
He who takes pleasure in tracing an etymology.
Two workmen playing, in a café in the South,
a silent game of chess.
The potter, contemplating a color and a form.
The typographer who sets this page well,
though it may not please him.
A woman and a man, who read the last tercets
of a certain canto.
He who strokes a sleeping animal.
He who justifies, or wishes to justify, a wrong done him.
He who is grateful for the existence of Stevenson.
He who prefers others to be right.
These people, unaware, are saving the world.
linesCn: 一个像伏尔泰希望的那样耕耘自己花园的人。
一个感激音乐存在的人。
一个乐于追溯词源的人。
两个工人在南方的咖啡馆里
默默下着一盘棋。
陶工凝视着一种颜色和一种形状。
排字工人把这一页排得很好，
虽然也许并不令他满意。
一个女人和一个男人在读某个篇章
最后的三行诗。
一个抚摸熟睡动物的人。
一个为别人对自己的伤害辩护或想要辩护的人。
一个感激斯蒂文森存在的人。
一个宁愿别人有理的人。
这些人，他们不知道，正在拯救世界。

title: Corona
lines: Autumn eats its leaf out of my hand: we are friends.
From the nuts we shell time and we teach it to walk:
then time returns to the shell.
In the mirror it's Sunday,
in dream there is room for sleeping,
our mouths speak the truth.
My eye moves down to the sex of my loved one:
we look at each other,
we exchange dark words,
we love each other like poppy and recollection,
we sleep like wine in the conches,
like the sea in the moon's blood ray.
We stand by the window embracing, and people look up from the street:
it is time they knew!
It is time the stone made an effort to flower,
time unrest had a beating heart.
It is time it were time.
It is time.
linesCn: 秋天从我手中吃它的叶子：我们是朋友。
我们从坚果中剥出时间并教它行走：
于是时间回到壳中。
镜中是星期天，
梦中有地方可以睡觉，
我们的嘴说着真话。
我的目光落到爱人的身体上：
我们互相凝视，
我们交换黑暗的词语，
我们相爱如罂粟和记忆，
我们沉睡如海螺中的酒，
如月亮血色光线中的大海。
我们拥抱着站在窗前，人们从街上望来：
是他们知道的时候了！
是石头努力开花的时候了，
是不安拥有一颗跳动之心的时候了。
是该是时候的时候了。
是时候了。

title: Ithaka
lines: As you set out for Ithaka,
hope the voyage is a long one,
full of adventure, full of discovery.
Laistrygonians and Cyclops,
angry Poseidon — don't be afraid of them:
you'll never find things like that on your way
as long as you keep your thoughts raised high,
as long as a rare excitement
stirs your spirit and your body.
Ithaka gave you the marvelous journey.
Without her you would not have set out.
She has nothing left to give you now.
And if you find her poor, Ithaka won't have fooled you.
Wise as you will have become, so full of experience,
you will have understood by then what these Ithakas mean.
linesCn: 当你启程前往伊萨卡，
但愿你的道路漫长，
充满奇迹，充满发现。
莱斯特律戈涅斯巨人，独眼巨人，
愤怒的波塞冬——不要怕他们：
你将不会在途中碰到诸如此类的东西，
只要你高扬你的思想，
只要有一种特殊的感觉
触动你的精神和肉体。
伊萨卡赐予你如此神奇的旅行。
没有她你不会出发。
她现在已没有什么可以给你的了。
而如果你发现她贫穷，伊萨卡并没有欺骗你。
你已如此智慧，如此富有经验，
你一定已经明白了这些伊萨卡意味着什么。

title: Tobacco Shop (excerpt)
lines: I'm nothing.
I'll always be nothing.
I can't want to be something.
But I have in me all the dreams of the world.
Windows of my room,
The room of one of the world's millions nobody knows
(And if they knew me, what would they know?),
You open onto the mystery of a street continually crossed by people,
A street inaccessible to any thought,
Real, impossibly real, certain, unknowingly certain,
With the mystery of things beneath the stones and beings.
linesCn: 我什么都不是。
我永远都不会是什么。
我不能想要成为什么。
但我心中有全世界的梦。
我房间的窗户，
世界上千百万无人知晓的人中一个人的房间的窗户
（即使他们认识我，他们又能知道什么？），
你们朝着一条不断有人穿过的街道的神秘敞开，
一条任何思想都无法抵达的街道，
真实的，不可能地真实的，确定的，不自知地确定的，
带着石头和存在之下事物的神秘。

title: The Panther
lines: His vision, from the constantly passing bars,
has grown so weary that it cannot hold
anything else. It seems to him there are
a thousand bars; and behind the bars, no world.
As he paces in cramped circles, over and over,
the movement of his powerful soft strides
is like a ritual dance around a center
in which a mighty will stands paralyzed.
Only at times, the curtain of the pupils
lifts, quietly—. An image enters in,
rushes down through the tensed, arrested muscles,
plunges into the heart and is gone.
linesCn: 它的目光被那走不完的铁栏
缠得这般疲倦，什么也不能收留。
它觉得只有千条的铁栏杆，
千条的铁栏后便没有宇宙。
强韧的脚步迈着柔软的步容，
步容在这极小的圈中旋转，
仿佛力之舞围绕着一个中心，
在中心一个伟大的意志昏眩。
只有时眼帘无声地撩起——
于是有一幅图像浸入，
通过四肢紧张的静寂——
在心中化为乌有。

title: Ode to the Sea
lines: Here on the island
the sea
and how much sea.
It overflows from time to time.
It says yes, then no,
then no, no, no,
then yes, in blue,
in foam, in gallop.
It says no, again no.
It cannot be still.
My name is sea, it repeats
striking a stone
but not convincing it.
linesCn: 在这座岛上
是海
多少海。
它不时溢出。
它说是，然后不，
然后不，不，不，
然后在蓝色中说是，
在泡沫中，在奔腾中。
它说不，又是不。
它无法静止。
我的名字是海，它重复着
拍打着一块石头
但没能说服它。

title: A Word on Statistics
lines: Out of every hundred people,
those who always know better: fifty-two.
Unsure of every step: almost all the rest.
Ready to help, if it doesn't take long: forty-nine.
Always good, because they can't be otherwise:
four — well, maybe five.
Able to admire without envy: eighteen.
Led to error
by youth (which passes):
sixty, give or take.
Those not to be messed with: four and forty.
Living in constant fear
of someone or something: seventy-seven.
Capable of happiness: twenty-some-odd at most.
Harmless alone,
turning savage in crowds: more than half, for sure.
linesCn: 每一百个人中，
什么都懂得更多的：五十二个。
对每一步都不确定的：几乎所有其余的人。
愿意帮忙，只要不太费事：四十九个。
总是善良的，因为不会别的：
四个——好吧，也许五个。
能欣赏而不嫉妒的：十八个。
被青春（它会过去的）
引入歧途的：
六十个，左右。
不好惹的：四十四个。
经常对某人或某事
感到恐惧的：七十七个。
能感到幸福的：至多二十几个。
独处时无害，
在人群中变得凶残的：肯定超过一半。

title: The Road Not Taken
lines: Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
To where it bent in the undergrowth;
Then took the other, as just as fair,
And having perhaps the better claim,
Because it was grassy and wanted wear;
Though as for that the passing there
Had worn them really about the same,
I shall be telling this with a sigh
Somewhere ages and ages hence:
Two roads diverged in a wood, and I—
I took the one less traveled by,
And that has made all the difference.
linesCn: 黄色的树林里分出两条路，
可惜我不能同时去涉足，
我在那路口久久伫立，
我向着一条路极目望去，
直到它消失在丛林深处。
但我选了另外一条路，
它荒草萋萋，十分幽寂，
显得更诱人，更美丽；
虽然在这条小路上，
都很少留下旅人的足迹。
也许多少年后在某个地方，
我将轻声叹息将往事回顾：
一片树林里分出两条路——
而我选择了人迹更少的一条，
从此决定了我一生的道路。

title: When You Are Old
lines: When you are old and grey and full of sleep,
And nodding by the fire, take down this book,
And slowly read, and dream of the soft look
Your eyes had once, and of their shadows deep;
How many loved your moments of glad grace,
And loved your beauty with love false or true,
But one man loved the pilgrim soul in you,
And loved the sorrows of your changing face;
And bending down beside the glowing bars,
Murmur, a little sadly, how Love fled
And paced upon the mountains overhead
And hid his face amid a crowd of stars.
linesCn: 当你老了，头白了，睡意昏沉，
炉火旁打盹，请取下这部诗歌，
慢慢读，回想你过去眼神的柔和，
回想它们昔日浓重的阴影；
多少人爱你青春欢畅的时辰，
爱慕你的美丽，假意或真心，
只有一个人爱你那朝圣者的灵魂，
爱你衰老了的脸上痛苦的皱纹；
垂下头来，在红光闪耀的炉子旁，
凄然地轻轻诉说那爱情的消逝，
在头顶的山上它缓缓踱着步子，
在一群星星中间隐藏着脸庞。

title: Because I could not stop for Death
titleCn: 因为我不能停步等候死神
lines: Because I could not stop for Death –
He kindly stopped for me –
The Carriage held but just Ourselves –
And Immortality.
We slowly drove – He knew no haste
And I had put away
My labor and my leisure too,
For His Civility –
We passed the School, where Children strove
At Recess – in the Ring –
We passed the Fields of Gazing Grain –
We passed the Setting Sun –
Since then – 'tis Centuries – and yet
Feels shorter than the Day
I first surmised the Horses' Heads
Were toward Eternity –
linesCn: 因为我不能停步等候死神——
他便好心停车等我——
车厢里只容得下我们两个——
还有永生。
我们缓缓而行——他不知匆忙
而我已经把
劳作和闲暇都放到一旁，
感谢他的殷勤——
我们经过学校，孩子们
在课间——围成圆圈争闹——
我们经过注目凝望的田野——
我们经过落日——
从那以后——已过数百年——却觉得
比那一天还短
我最初猜想到马头
朝向的是永恒——

title: Song of the Open Road (excerpt)
lines: Afoot and light-hearted I take to the open road,
Healthy, free, the world before me,
The long brown path before me leading wherever I choose.
Henceforth I ask not good-fortune, I myself am good-fortune,
Henceforth I whimper no more, postpone no more, need nothing,
Done with indoor complaints, libraries, querulous criticisms,
Strong and content I travel the open road.
linesCn: 我轻松愉快地走上大路，
我健康，我自由，整个世界展开在我面前，
漫长的褐色大路在前方引我到想去的任何地方。
从今以后我不再要求幸运，我自己就是幸运，
从今以后我不再哭泣、推迟、也不需要什么，
不再理会室内的抱怨、书本和不满的批评，
强壮而满足，我走上大路。

title: The Albatross
lines: Often, to amuse themselves, the men of a crew
Catch albatrosses, those vast sea birds
That indolently follow a ship
As it glides over the deep, briny sea.
Scarcely have they placed them on the deck
Than these kings of the sky, clumsy, ashamed,
Pitifully let their great white wings
Drag beside them like oars.
The Poet is like this prince of cloud and storm
Who haunts the tempest and laughs at the archer;
Exiled on the ground in the midst of jeers,
His giant wings prevent him from walking.
linesCn: 水手们常常为了消遣，
捉住信天翁，那些巨大的海鸟，
它们懒洋洋地伴随着航船，
在苦涩的深渊上面滑翔。
刚被放到甲板上面，
这些碧空之王，羞怯而笨拙，
可怜地垂下了它们
白色的大翅膀，像两支桨拖在身旁。
诗人就好似这位云中的君主，
出没于暴风雨中，嘲笑弓箭手；
一旦被放逐在地上，遭人嘲弄，
他的巨翅却使他寸步难行。

title: Sensation
lines: On blue summer evenings, I shall go down the paths,
Getting pricked by the corn, crushing the short grass:
In a dream I shall feel its coolness on my feet.
I shall let the wind bathe my bare head.
I shall not speak, I shall think about nothing:
But endless love will mount in my soul;
And I shall travel far, very far, like a gypsy,
Through the countryside — as happy as if I were with a woman.
linesCn: 在蓝色的夏日黄昏，我将走上小径，
被麦芒刺痛，踩着细碎的草：
梦想中我感到脚下的清凉。
我让风沐浴我裸露的头。
我将不再说话，也不再思索：
但无尽的爱将涌上我的灵魂；
我将远行，走很远很远，像一个流浪者，
穿过大自然——幸福得像身旁有个女人。

title: Somnambule Ballad (excerpt)
lines: Green, how I want you green.
Green wind. Green branches.
The ship out on the sea
and the horse on the mountain.
With the shadow on her waist
she dreams on her balcony,
green flesh, green hair,
with eyes of cold silver.
Green, how I want you green.
Under the gypsy moon,
things are looking at her
and she cannot look back.
linesCn: 绿啊，我多么爱你这绿色。
绿的风，绿的枝条。
船在海上，
马在山中。
影子缠在腰间，
她在阳台上做梦，
绿色的肌肤，绿色的头发，
和冷银色的眼睛。
绿啊，我多么爱你这绿色。
在吉卜赛人的月亮下，
万物都在注视着她，
而她无法回望。

title: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock (excerpt)
titleCn: 普鲁弗洛克的情歌（节选）
lines: Let us go then, you and I,
When the evening is spread out against the sky
Like a patient etherized upon a table;
Let us go, through certain half-deserted streets,
The muttering retreats
Of restless nights in one-night cheap hotels
And sawdust restaurants with oyster-shells:
Streets that follow like a tedious argument
Of insidious intent
To lead you to an overwhelming question ...
Oh, do not ask, “What is it?”
Let us go and make our visit.
linesCn: 那么我们走吧，你我两个人，
正当朝天空慢慢铺展着黄昏
好似病人麻醉在手术台上；
我们走吧，穿过几条半清冷的街，
那些喃喃自语的去处
有廉价小旅馆中一夜不安的睡眠
和满地锯末与牡蛎壳的饭馆；
街连着街，像一场冗长的争论
带着阴险的意图
把你引向一个难以抗拒的问题……
哦，别问，“那是什么？”
让我们快去做客吧。

title: Funeral Blues
lines: Stop all the clocks, cut off the telephone,
Prevent the dog from barking with a juicy bone,
Silence the pianos and with muffled drum
Bring out the coffin, let the mourners come.
He was my North, my South, my East and West,
My working week and my Sunday rest,
My noon, my midnight, my talk, my song;
I thought that love would last for ever: I was wrong.
The stars are not wanted now: put out every one;
Pack up the moon and dismantle the sun;
Pour away the ocean and sweep up the wood;
For nothing now can ever come to any good.
linesCn: 停止所有的时钟，切断电话，
给狗一根肉骨头别让它叫嚷，
让钢琴沉默，在低沉的鼓声中
抬出灵柩，让吊唁者前来。
他是我的北，我的南，我的东和西，
我的工作日，我的休息天，
我的正午，我的子夜，我的话语，我的歌；
我以为爱会永恒：我错了。
不再需要星星了：把每一颗都熄灭；
把月亮包起来，拆掉太阳；
倒掉海洋，扫除森林；
因为现在什么也不会有好的结果了。

title: Gift
lines: A day so happy.
Fog lifted early, I worked in the garden.
Hummingbirds were stopping over honeysuckle flowers.
There was no thing on earth I wanted to possess.
I knew no one worth my envying him.
Whatever evil I had suffered, I forgot.
To think that once I was the same man did not embarrass me.
In my body I felt no pain.
When straightening up, I saw the blue sea and sails.
linesCn: 如此幸福的一天。
雾早早地散了，我在花园里干活。
蜂鸟停在忍冬花上。
这世上没有一样东西我想占有。
我知道没有一个人值得我羡慕。
任何我曾遭受的不幸，我都忘了。
想到故我今我同为一人并不使我难为情。
在我身体里没有痛苦。
直起腰来，我望见蓝色的大海和帆影。

title: Track
lines: 2 A.M.: moonlight. The train has stopped
out in a field. Far off sparks of light from a town,
flickering coldly on the horizon.
As when a man goes so deep into his dream
he will never remember that he was there
when he returns again to his room.
Or when a person goes so deep into a sickness
that his days all become some flickering sparks, a swarm,
feeble and cold on the horizon.
The train is entirely motionless.
2 o'clock: strong moonlight, few stars.
linesCn: 凌晨两点：月光。火车停下了
在一片旷野上。远处城镇的光
在地平线上冷冷闪烁。
就像一个人走进他的梦如此之深
以至回到房间里后
再也记不起他到过那里。
又像一个人走进疾病如此之深
他的日子全变成一些闪烁的火花，一群
微弱而冰冷的火花，在地平线上。
火车完全静止不动。
两点钟：浓重的月光，稀少的星。

title: El Desdichado
lines: I am the dark one, — the widower, — the unconsoled,
The prince of Aquitaine at the abolished tower:
My sole star is dead, — and my constellated lute
Bears the black sun of Melancholia.
In the night of the tomb, you who consoled me,
Give me back Posilipo and the Italian sea,
The flower which so pleased my stricken heart,
And the trellis where the vine and the rose are entwined.
linesCn: 我是那幽暗的人，——丧偶的，——无可慰藉的，
阿基坦的王子立于被废的塔楼：
我唯一的星已死去，——我缀满星的琴
背负着忧郁的黑太阳。
在坟墓之夜，你这安慰过我的人，
把波西利波和意大利的海还给我，
那朵曾使我悲伤的心欢愉的花，
和葡萄藤与蔷薇缠绕的棚架。

title: Requiem (excerpt)
lines: No, not under the vault of alien skies,
And not under the shelter of alien wings —
I was with my people then,
There, where my people, unfortunately, were.
In those years only the dead could smile,
Glad to be at rest.
And Leningrad city swayed like
A needless appendage around its prisons.
linesCn: 不，不是在异乡的天穹下，
也不是在异乡的羽翼的庇护下——
那时我和我的人民在一起，
在那里，在我的人民不幸所在的地方。
在那些年月里只有死人是微笑的，
为终得安息而庆幸。
而列宁格勒像一个无用的附属物
在它的监狱周围摇晃。

title: The Graveyard by the Sea (excerpt)
lines: This quiet roof, where dove-sails saunter by,
Between the pines, the tombs, throbs visibly.
Impartial noon patterns the sea in flame —
That sea forever starting and re-starting.
When thought has had its hour, oh how rewarding
Are the long vistas of celestial calm!
The wind is rising! ... We must try to live!
The huge air opens and shuts my book;
The wave dares to explode out of the rocks
In reeking cascades of spray!
linesCn: 这片平静的屋顶上有白鸽荡漾，
它在松林和坟墓间颤动，可以望见。
公正的正午用火焰织出了大海——
大海啊，永远在重新开始！
思想经历了漫长的沉思之后，
多好啊，放眼远眺那天神的宁静！
起风了！……唯有试着活下去！
无边的空气翻开又合上我的书；
波浪敢于从岩石上
飞溅起刺目的浪花！

title: On Living
lines: Living is no laughing matter:
you must live with great seriousness
like a squirrel, for example —
I mean without looking for something beyond and above living,
I mean living must be your whole occupation.
Living is no laughing matter:
you must take it seriously,
so much so and to such a degree
that, for example, your hands tied behind your back,
your back to the wall,
or else in a laboratory
in your white coat and safety glasses,
you can die for people —
even for people whose faces you've never seen,
even though you know living
is the most real, the most beautiful thing.
linesCn: 活着不是闹着玩的：
你必须非常认真地活着，
就像一只松鼠，比方说——
我的意思是，不要在生活之外去寻找什么，
我的意思是，活着必须是你全部的事业。
活着不是闹着玩的：
你必须严肃对待它，
严肃到这样的程度——
比方说，双手被反绑在背后，
背靠着墙，
或者在一间实验室里
穿着白大褂戴着护目镜，
你可以为人们而死——
甚至为那些你从未见过面的人，
尽管你知道活着
是最真实、最美丽的事情。

title: February
lines: February. Get ink, shed tears.
Write of it, sob your heart out, sing,
While torrential slush that roars
Burns in the blackness of the spring.
Get a cab. For six grivnas,
Race through the clang of bells and wheels
To where the ink and all you grieving
Are a downpour louder than your tears.
Where, like charred pears, a myriad rooks,
Torn from the trees, plunge into puddles,
Hurling dry sadness to the depths
Of eyes where pools of water struggle.
Beneath it all, the earth is black,
Spring's in the air; and the more loudly
It comes, the more believable
When verse grows out of it, in sobs.
linesCn: 二月。墨水足够用来痛哭！
大放悲声抒写二月，
一直到轰响的泥泞
燃起黑色的春天。
用六戈比，雇辆马车，
穿过钟声，穿过轮响，
赶到那墨水和悲痛
都被暴雨淹没的地方。
在那里，像烧焦的梨子，
成千的白嘴鸦
从树上落下，跌进水洼，
把干枯的忧愁沉入眼底。
眼底下，雪融化处泛着黑色，
风被呐喊声翻遍，
越是偶然，便越真实，
越是痛哭着编成诗篇。

title: The Envoy of Mr. Cogito
lines: Go where those others went to the dark boundary
for the golden fleece of nothingness — your last prize
go upright among those who are on their knees
among those with their backs turned and those toppled in the dust
you were saved not in order to live
you have little time you must give testimony
be courageous when the mind deceives you be courageous
in the final account only this is important
and do not forgive truly it is not in your power
to forgive in the name of those betrayed at dawn
beware however of unnecessary pride
keep looking at your clown's face in the mirror
repeat: I was called — weren't there better ones than I
linesCn: 到那些人去过的地方去，到黑暗的边界去
去寻找虚无的金羊毛——你最后的奖赏
在那些跪着的人中间直立地走
在那些背过身去的人和倒在尘土中的人中间
你被拯救不是为了活着
你没有多少时间了你必须作证
当头脑欺骗你时要勇敢 要勇敢
在最后的结算中只有这才重要
不要宽恕 的确那不在你的权力之内
以黎明时被出卖的人的名义去宽恕
但要当心不必要的骄傲
在镜子里注视你的小丑的脸
重复：我被召唤了——难道没有比我更好的人吗
